name: Tracing Shahjahanabad  - A Journey in Old Delhi
description: Old Delhi is a labyrinth that demands more than a glance—it requires an exploration. Ahead of the grand facade of the Red Fort lies a dense, vibrant tapestry of urban life that has pulsed uninterrupted for nearly four hundred years. Beyond the famous landmarks, we explore the living heritage and the spiritual harmony of its famous landmarks standing side-by-side, navigating the legendary thoroughfare of Chandni Chowk, before disappearing into the enchanting maze of its inner neighbourhoods—a kaleidoscope of commerce and culture.Join us for a morning of storytelling as we traverse our way through its Katra’s and Kutcha’s, via old gateways, looking into old Haveli’s to explore an imperial city planned by Emperor Shah Jahan and a market designed by Princess Jahanara. Note: There is no charge for accompanying children below 12.

name: Mandi House Heritage Walk: A Cultural Microcosm
description: There are places in Delhi that tell the story of power.And then there are places that tell the story of imagination.Join us for a unique heritage walk through Mandi House — Delhi’s extraordinary cultural district and perhaps the closest thing India has to a living artistic republic.This walk explores how, in the decades after Independence, a remarkable vision began taking shape in the heart of the capital: a space where theatre, dance, music, literature, visual arts, cinema, debate, and experimentation could flourish side by side.From the 1950s and 60s onwards, through political support, institutional ambition, and the dreams of generations of artists, Mandi House slowly transformed into a cultural microcosm of India.As we walk through this fascinating neighbourhood, we will uncover the stories behind some of India’s most iconic cultural institutions:From the elegance of Shri Ram Bharatiya Kala Kendra and the legendary Shri Ram Centre for Performing Arts,to the historic Kamani Auditorium and the National School of Drama,from Triveni Kala Sangam and LTG Auditorium,to Sapru House, FICCI Auditorium, the Russian Centre, Modern School, and the beloved Bengali Market.But this is not merely a walk about buildings.It is a walk about ideas.About the making of modern India through culture.About artists, playwrights, dancers, architects, bureaucrats, politicians, and visionaries who together imagined a capital city where art would not remain confined to palaces or elites, but become part of public life itself.We will also trace the older history of the area and uncover an intriguing question:Why is it called *Mandi House*?Who built it?What existed here before this cultural district emerged?And how did this part of Delhi become the beating heart of India’s modern performing arts movement?Expect stories of rehearsals and revolutions, poetry and politics, architecture and activism, cafés and canteens, backstage struggles and artistic triumphs — all woven into the landscape of one of Delhi’s most intellectually alive neighbourhoods.Come experience Mandi House not merely as a location,but as an idea.📍 Venue: Mandi House🕰 Duration: Approx. 2 to 2.5 hours👣 Conducted by: Darwesh Heritage WalksLimited slots only.

name: The Algorithm as Therapist
description: Join us for a lecture that explores a subtle shift in our inner lives: the moment we stopped turning to people for understanding and started turning to code. About the LectureA "good" therapy session is often messy. It involves sitting with ambiguity, tracing complex relationships, and allowing contradictions to unfold. But we now live in a "Rapido" world that prizes speed and efficiency. Today, AI chatbots offer instant validation, neat summaries, and structured ways of processing our emotions.For many, these tools serve the "missing middle," providing support for those who need more than a wellness app but cannot access formal care. However, as we increasingly lean on algorithms, we begin to privilege the parts of ourselves that are rational and productive. Emotional messiness starts to feel like a "bug" to be fixed rather than a human experience to be understood.The session explores:How AI is filling the gap in mental health care and what it offers the modern user.Why turning to algorithms encourages us to be coherent and productive at the expense of our complex, "messy" selves.Which ways of understanding our pain are being amplified by technology, and which are being lost?What it means to maintain psychological depth in an age of automated empathy.At its core, this talk asks: as the algorithm becomes our therapist, what kind of selves are we being encouraged to become?About the SpeakerAnnie Baxi is a mental health practitioner, qualitative researcher, and Visiting Professor at Ashoka University and BML University. Her work focuses on critical and cultural approaches to psychology, questioning mainstream notions of "normalcy," health, and wellness.With a background that bridges academic rigor and clinical practice, Annie explores how culture and technology intersect with our well-being. Beyond her professional work, she is a devoted pet parent and a collector of picture storybooks, believing that some of the most profound human truths are often found in the simplest forms.Ticket IncludesAll Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Luxury Uncovered: The Hidden Stories Behind Brands
description: Join us for a session that pulls back the curtain on the world of luxury, moving beyond the logos and price tags to reveal the messy, accidental, and fascinating realities that build iconic brands. About the LectureLuxury is often presented to us as a narrative of effortless perfection and heritage. But beneath the polished surface lies a deeper reality shaped by constraints, cultural shifts, and strategic improvisations that are rarely discussed in public.This session explores the "unseen" side of some of the world's most famous fashion houses. From chance encounters that birthed legendary products to functional innovations that were engineered into symbols of aspiration, we will uncover how luxury isn't just about what is made, but how the mythology around it is constructed. The session explores:The Architecture of Myth: How brands transform accidental origins into timeless stories.Engineered Scarcity: The strategic intent behind making products difficult to find.The Improvisation Factor: How constraints and unexpected inspirations lead to iconic creations.The Intersection of Creative & Commerce: How luxury continues to evolve through an interplay of culture and storytelling.Whether you are a creative professional or simply curious about the business of desire, this talk offers a fresh lens into the forces that truly shape the modern luxury landscape.Flow of the Evening4:00 PM – Doors open: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable.4:05 – 4:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations.4:30 PM: The learning begins.5:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Divam.5:30 PM onwards – Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, and more conversations.About the SpeakerDivam Jain is a Division Leader at Pearl Academy School of Business, where he leads programs at the intersection of fashion, luxury, and the creative industries. Educated both in India and abroad, he brings a global perspective to creative business education, blending diverse cultural insights with modern industry practices.Divam's work focuses on reimagining luxury education by exploring consumer psychology, storytelling, and the impact of AI on branding. Known for his insight-driven sessions, he has led immersive collaborations with global brands, helping students and professionals navigate the real-world challenges of the business of creativity.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Rethinking Work in Midlife
description: Not sure a full-time role fits anymore but not done working either?Fractional roles offer a way to stay engaged, take on meaningful responsibilities, and use your experience across organisations — without being tied to one place.What does it mean?Working differently, not less.Some people don’t want to stop working.They just don’t want to do it full-time in one place.A fractional role is when you take on a defined leadership or advisory responsibility within an organisation that needs your experience — but not in a full-time, single-role structure.You work across one or more organisations, contributing where it matters most.What to expectA 60-minute moderated conversation with professionals currently working as fractional CXOs.You will hear:Where they were earlier in their careersWhat led them to explore fractional rolesWhat their work looks like nowWhat has been harder than expectedWhat didn’t work initiallyFollowed by an open conversation and Q&A.Who this is forExperienced professionals rethinking full-time roles and exploring more flexible, meaningful ways of working.Format60-minute online sessionModerated by Sanjay Gopinath

name: In Her Interest by Zerodha - Delhi
description: Women have a complicated relationship with money. And it starts early. Many families shield their daughters from anything related to ‘finance’, but this well-meaning protection ends up being a disservice. For when these young women step into the real world, they lack the confidence to make financial decisions - not because they’re incapable, but because they were never given information on budgeting, savings, and investments. Later in life, many continue to rely on their partners for investment decisions. This dependency on others will only stop when they have the right information and toolkit to take back control. That’s where we come in.We launch 'In Her Interest' on 22nd May in Delhi and will focus on one core idea for the launch:Building financial security for women.The session will be led by two amazing experts who truly get it.Zeba Zaidi Adeeb, Co-Founder - Spectacom Global, Creator of Devils Circuit & The Yoddha RaceManisha Pande, Tax Partner, Aeka Advisors (ex - GE, AA/EY)This session is on  Finance and the Entrepreneur.Once you buy a ticket, you also get to send a question that the speakers will address during the session.Note: Each session is theme-based, this session is about 'Finance and the Entrepreneur' - we are not talking stocks and shares.

name: #lecture31: How Not To Be Fooled By Data
description: India is often described through abstractions.A billion people. A growing economy. A young country. A democracy of dizzying complexity.But somewhere between these familiar descriptions lies another India. One hiding quietly inside numbers, maps, charts, and patterns we rarely stop to examine closely.The next 100 babies born in India will not arrive evenly across the country. Some languages are disappearing while others coexist within the same neighbourhood. Millions describe themselves as employed in ways economists struggle to classify. Entire assumptions about food habits collapse once data replaces perception.And sometimes, statistics reveal stories stranger than fiction.Data is often treated as cold, technical, and inaccessible. Yet the best data has always done something more ambitious. It helps us notice. It exposes contradictions, unsettles assumptions, and occasionally changes how we understand ourselves.This session invites you into that process of discovery.Through surprises, puzzles, myth-busting facts, and visually engaging charts, the lecture will move across questions that are at once playful and deeply revealing:Where will the next 100 babies in India be born? Which is India’s most multilingual city? Why is almost nobody in India considered jobless after 29? Why does a banker contribute far more to GDP than a farmer? What is the perception-versus-reality gap between vegetarian and non-vegetarian India? And are SRK’s and Rajinikanth’s future heroines even born yet?The evening will mix insight with curiosity, humour with analysis, and information with surprise. Expect twists, unexpected connections, and charts that feel less like presentations and more like treasure maps.At a moment when information is abundant but attention is fragmented, perhaps the rarest experience is learning to see familiar things differently.About the SpeakerRohit Saran is the Managing Editor of The Times of India. Over the course of his career, he has served as Executive Editor at The Times of India, The Economic Times, and India Today, and was previously Editor-in-Chief of Business Today and Khaleej Times.Since the 1990s, he has worked extensively in data and visual journalism, combining numbers, visuals, and narrative to build richer forms of storytelling across some of India’s leading newsrooms.Earlier this year, Rohit co-authored 100 Ways to See India - Stats, Stories and Surprises with Sajeev Kumarapuram, Design Editor at The Times of India. Together, they have spent years navigating India’s data landscape searching for elusive numbers, surviving endless PDFs, and uncovering stories hidden inside statistics.Book now & develop your own pint of view! 🍻📌 Note: Alcohol will be served only to guests aged 25 and above. Non-alcoholic options will be available. Attendance implies consent to being filmed for event documentation.

location.address: Eldeco Centre, Block A, Shivalik Colony, Malviya Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi 110017, India

name: When Power Claims Victimhood
description: Join us for an evening of unpacking how the language of equality is being reshaped in India’s courts, and what it means when power begins to claim victimhood.About the LectureWe often think of equality as a fixed principle, clear, neutral, and universally agreed upon. But what if the definition of equality itself is being redefined in real time?Drawing from landmark cases and ongoing constitutional debates, this lecture explores the idea of equality under the Indian Constitution and how it is being contested today. At its core is a provocative question, what happens when historically dominant groups begin to frame themselves as victims?Through a close reading of recent litigation, the session examines how legal arguments are reframing structural advantage as disadvantage, and how courts are becoming arenas where these competing narratives are negotiated.Moving across themes of caste, religion, gender, and institutional power, the lecture explores:Power and Victimhood: How dominant groups are increasingly adopting the language of historical injustice and reparationsEquality as Transformation: Why the Constitution envisioned equality not as neutrality, but as a tool to address deep-rooted structural inequalitiesThe Courtroom as Battleground: How contemporary legal disputes, from reservations to religious rights, are reshaping constitutional meaningReverse Discrimination Debates: The rise of arguments that position corrective measures as unfair to historically privileged groupsWhat’s at Stake: How these shifts could redefine the very foundation of India’s constitutional projectFlow of the Evening5:00 PM: Doors open at Social, Saket: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable5:00 – 5:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations5:30 PM: The learning begins6:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Ms. Wadekar6:30 PM onwards: Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversationsAbout the SpeakerMs. Disha Wadekar is a constitutional lawyer with over a decade of litigation experience in India. An LL.M. graduate from Columbia Law School and a Fulbright scholar, her work sits at the intersection of law, social justice, and structural inequality.She has been involved in several significant constitutional cases, including the Sabarimala temple entry case and the EWS reservation case. She has represented the families of Rohith Vemula and Payal Tadvi in their fight against institutional discrimination, as well as journalist Sukanya Shantha in a petition challenging caste-based division of labour in Indian prisons. In 2018, she was the only woman lawyer representing Dalit victims before the Bhima Koregaon Judicial Inquiry Commission.In 2022, she was appointed Additional Special Public Prosecutor in a case concerning the rape of a Dalit girl in Rajasthan.Beyond litigation, Ms. Wadekar has taught Constitutional Law and Critical Legal Studies at National Law University, Delhi and O.P. Jindal Global University. She is also the co-founder of CEDE (Community for the Eradication of Discrimination in Education and Employment), an organisation working to increase representation of marginalised communities in the legal profession and judiciary.Her work extends to legal research and consultancy with institutions such as the Centre for Women’s Development Studies and Project 39A, and her writing has been featured in leading journals and platforms.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

description: This May, join us for a special edition of the Old Delhi City Walk, exploring the haunting histories and layered legends of Khooni Darwaza and Feroz Shah Kotla Fort.Led by young guides from Salaam Baalak Trust, the walk offers a rare perspective on the city beyond the usual tourist trail — before concluding with a breakfast buffet at Chor Bizarre.Meeting Point: Delhi Gate Metro Station (Gate 1) at 7:30AM sharp

name: The Listening Room (for members)
description: The Listening Room: A Space for Deep ListeningOverviewIn a world that is always talking, we rarely get the chance to truly listen. The Listening Room is a unique session dedicated to the art of being present. It is a space to share, to hear others, and to practice the kind of attention that builds real connection and empathy.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectActive Listening: Practice being fully present while others speak without the need to interrupt or give immediate advice.Shared Stories: A safe environment to share your own thoughts or experiences and feel truly heard by a small group.Quiet Reflection: Moments of silence to process what has been shared and to notice how deep listening changes our perspective.Supportive Connection: A morning at Greenr focused on human presence and the simple power of witnessing each other’s stories.Details🗓️ Date: 19th April (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:00 AM📍 Location: Greenr, Golf Course Ext Road

name: SCC Gourmet Cinema - Midnight in Paris
description: Sunset Cinema Club, in collaboration with The Piano Man New Delhi, invites you to a moveable feast through time, where nostalgia, romance, and timeless flavors transport you to a Paris that exists somewhere between memory and imagination.Inspired by Midnight in Paris, this experience follows Gil Pender’s whimsical journey through the streets of Paris, where past and present blur, and every midnight unlocks a different era. As he drifts from modern day Paris to the golden age of the 1920s, and further into the dreamlike charm of La Belle Époque, the film becomes an ode to art, longing, and the magic of another time.Experience this journey through a thoughtfully curated 5-course tasting menu inspired by Paris through time, from modern day Paris to the roaring 1920s, culminating in the elegance of La Belle Époque. Each course brings together refined classics, nostalgic indulgences, and playful reinterpretations, served in sync with moments on screen and echoing the film’s themes of romance, reinvention, and the allure of the past.Food: Vegetarian and Non-Vegetarian options available.Drinks: 1 Alcoholic and 1 Non-Alcoholic beverage available.Please note: The menu is fixed and cannot be modified.Seating is pre-arranged. If you’ve booked a ticket, you are assured a seat.

name: The Politics of Motherhood
description: Join us on Sunday, May 24, for a conversation that asks a question we’ve long internalised but rarely examined closely: What if the way we talk about mothers isn’t just cultural, but deeply political?About the LectureMotherhood is often framed as instinctive, natural, and deeply personal. But beneath these ideas lies a powerful and persistent narrative, one that places the burden of blame, responsibility, and perfection squarely on mothers.This session explores how motherhood has been shaped by cultural expectations that are often contradictory and impossible to meet. A mother is told she loves too much or too little, is too present or not present enough. Working mothers are scrutinised, while those who stay at home are diminished. Even the choice to not become a mother carries its own weight of judgment.At its core, this lecture reframes these experiences, not as individual failings, but as part of a larger system.The session explores:The Blame Narrative: How mothers have historically been positioned as responsible for outcomes beyond their controlCultural Contradictions: The impossible standards placed on women across different forms of motherhoodLanguage as Power: How psychological and cultural terms, from ‘mother wound’ to attachment styles, can unintentionally reinforce blameInternalised Shame: How these narratives shape the way mothers see themselves, and each otherRewriting the Story: What it means to view motherhood through a lens of context, care, and social structures rather than individual faultAbout the SpeakerDr. Shelja Sen is a Narrative & Family Therapist, writer, and Co-founder of Children First. She is a TED speaker, a columnist with The Indian Express, and the author of three critically acclaimed books: All You Need is Love, Imagine, and Reclaim Your Life.She serves as a Clinical Tutor at the University of Melbourne and is the Editor-in-Chief of the International Journal of Narrative Therapy & Community Work. Her work is rooted in building culturally aligned, ethical practices that bring together an intersectional lens, social justice, and innovation.At heart, she is a storyteller, someone deeply invested in how the stories we inherit shape the lives we live.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Why Did Bengal Vote Differently? | Policy & Chai
description: Every month, we pick one topic — a policy decision, a political shift, a global event — and actually talk it through. No lectures, no expertise required. Just curious people, different perspectives, and chai.This month's question: Why does Bengal vote differently from the rest of India?From the Left Front's 34-year reign to Mamata's TMC dominance, Bengal has consistently defied national political trends. What makes it so distinct — is it history, identity, class politics, or something else entirely? And what does it mean for 2026?What to expectA moderated discussion where everyone gets to speak. We go through different layers of the topic — historical context, current dynamics, what's at stake — and open it up for the room to debate, disagree, and figure things out together.No slides. No panel. Just a good conversation.Who's it forAnyone who's curious about Indian politics, follows the news, or just wants a space to think out loud with people who take these things seriously. You don't need to know Bengal politics inside out — half the fun is figuring it out together in the room.Join the communityWant to stay in the loop for future sessions? Join the Policy & Chai WhatsApp community here: https://chat.whatsapp.com/KHdTLqcmEaz53DBy1gSsB6Details📅 Sunday, 24th May⏰ 5:00 PM onwards📍 Siyah Art House, New Delhi🎟️ ₹219

name: Period Party 2.0 Delhi
description: Period Party is a deliberately intimate, community-led gathering held on World Menstrual Hygiene Day (May 28), designed to reclaim menstruation as a topic of open conversation, celebration, and solidarity. Period Party 2.0— a radical, unapologetic, bold, joyful, and inclusive gathering that reimagines how we talk, learn, and feel about our bodies and menstruation. Through creativity, shared stories, and reflection, we hope to create a space that feels safe, supportive, and stigma-free. Expect an insightful panels, a reflective workshop, sharing circles, poetry and collective feminist art. It is something you have never witnessed before!

Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to ExpectDeep Work for Reading: We will have a longer and silent reading block to help everyone actually get into a flow state.The "Currently Reading" Wall: A quick and casual way to share what everyone is reading and swap recommendations with the group.Slow Morning: No themes or prompts this time. name: Fresh Pasta Workshop
description: Join Anita in her rooftop kitchen-studio to make fresh pasta (eggless!!) from scratch and never buy pasta again.Italy does not have a monopoly on pasta - we have our own shapes! We'll learn how to make flat pasta as well as hand-formed shapes like Cavatelli and Farfalle (Italy), Shunali (Spiti), Sundarkala (Uttarakhand), and Skyu (from Ladakh), using basic pasta dough (including whole wheat). We will discuss different flour blends and understand how they influence methods and techniques for rolling and shaping pasta. While a rolling pin is all we need, we will also try our hands at using a pasta machine.At the end of the workshop we will gather some herbs from the garden, cook up what we've created, and sit down to enjoy the meal, and share food stories around the table.It's a hands-on workshop; dress comfortably.250+ workshops and counting! Anita is a practicing Landscape Architect who enjoys cooking with seasonal ingredients. Her Home and Garden, exemplifying sustainable living, have featured in leading media (HT, The Better India, etc). Her recipes have been published in leading journals and cookbooks. Like-minded people join her in the kitchen on weekends to cook and eat. She has been baking sourdough breads at home for over 15 years using local flours and her lively wild yeast starter. Come for Pasta, stay for the Bread.

name: Why We Taste With Our Ears
description: This lecture promises to be an evening that rethinks something as simple as eating, by tuning to a sense we rarely notice at the table.About the LectureWhy does the sound of rain make fried food more irresistible? Why does music change how we experience a glass of wine?This session begins with a simple but surprising idea: taste doesn’t start in the mouth, it often begins in the ear.Drawing on extensive research by Bruite Magazine across the subcontinent, this lecture explores how sound quietly shapes the way we anticipate, experience, and remember food. From the rhythm of a bustling street to the curated playlists of a fine-dining restaurant, auditory cues influence everything from perceived texture to indulgence and value.Moving across everyday eating environments and contemporary food spaces, the session explores:The Sound of Anticipation: How auditory cues shape cravings even before the first biteSonic Texture: Why crunch, sizzle, and silence can alter how we perceive flavour and freshnessAtmosphere & Memory: How music, noise, and rhythm influence mood and the way meals stay with usDesigning Food Experiences: The role of sound in restaurants, bars, supper clubs, and immersive dining formatsBlending cultural observation, field research, and sharp storytelling, this session offers a fresh lens on something deeply familiar.Flow of the Evening5:00 PM: Doors open at Siyah Arthouse, Saket: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable5:00 – 5:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations5:30 PM: The learning begins6:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Ms. Ramya6:30 PM onwards: Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversationsAbout the SpeakerRamya G Sri is the Co-founder and Editor-in-Chief of Bruite Magazine, one of South Asia’s leading independent food and culture publications known for its deeply researched, original storytelling and on-ground reporting.Through Bruite, she collaborates with chefs, historians, artisans, and communities across the subcontinent to explore how food intersects with culture, memory, and perception—often through unexpected and underexplored lenses.She also co-leads Bruite Media Co., a specialised brand advisory that has worked with over 500 brands and founders across F&B, lifestyle, D2C, and consumer tech. Her work spans brand launches, communication strategy, and creative training—shaping how brands tell stories and connect with audiences.Her contributions to storytelling have been recognised with the Laadli Media Award for Gender-Sensitive Storytelling and the Abby Silver Award for Advertising. Across her work, she brings together rigorous research, editorial depth, and a strong instinct for how narrative shapes both culture and consumer experience.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Unscript 2.0: Improv Comedy Show
description: After 14 hours of high-energy, no-holds-barred workshopping, this ensemble—now working at Level 2—steps into performance with sharper instincts, bolder choices, and a deepened sense of play. What unfolds is completely unscripted, wildly unpredictable, and built in the momenThis isn’t just a show you watch. It’s one you fuel.Your suggestions spark scenes. Your ideas twist the story. Your laughter drives the rhythm.A single word can spiral into a love story, a disaster, or something beautifully absurd. One performer holds the thread, the rest run with it—and somehow, chaos turns into magic.No scripts. No second takes. Just quick thinking, deep listening, and fearless fun.Expect moments that land perfectly, moments that go hilariously sideways, and everything in between. That’s where the real joy lives.HighlightsFully improvised, unscripted comedyAudience participation shapes the showPerformed by Level 2 trained ensembleHigh-energy, interactive experienceEvery show is completely uniqueAbout the HostAnshu DagaAn improv facilitator and performer guiding immersive, high-energy workshops that focus on spontaneity, presence, and creative expression.Come for the spontaneity. Stay for the surprises.Don’t plan the night—just secure your seat!

name: The Blue Moon Sound Bath
description: A rare celestial event. A shared energy. A real shift. The Immersion Room in collaboration with Eka by Paayal invites you into an immersive Blue Moon Sound Bath — a deeply calming evening designed to help you slow down, release emotional heaviness, and reconnect with yourself. Inspired by the symbolic energy of the Blue Moon, this experience becomes a gentle space for reflection, renewal, and inner stillness.As you arrive, you’ll be guided through grounding breathwork, intention setting, and a soothing meditation before sinking into a live sound immersion using healing instruments like sound bowls and chimes. The vibrations create a deeply restorative atmosphere that many describe as calming, expansive, and emotionally releasing.This is not just a meditation session — it is a collective pause from the noise of everyday life. A space where emotions soften, the mind quiets, and the body enters deep relaxation. Whether you are completely new to sound healing or already familiar with wellness practices, the experience is designed to feel accessible, nurturing, and immersive.Come as you are. Leave feeling lighter.
